scenarios.title: The connection registry represents N independent operator-global provider connections (Ollama + Anthropic + OpenAI + Microsoft Foundry/Azure + Google + Amazon Bedrock) declared in config/smackerel.yaml llm.connections[]; each is recorded as an independent slot (id, kind, non-secret params) with NO actor_user_id (operator-global, single shared graph), and the registry build is closed-set fail-loud on an unknown kind

Each provider kind records ONLY the non-secret connection parameters its kind requires (ollama base_url; openai org?/base_url?; azure-foundry endpoint/api_version/deployment; google vertex project/location or gemini none; bedrock region) carried generically by params without growing a contract field per kind; a missing required per-kind param fails loud naming the offending connection + param, and a kind records its own params, not another kind's

Missing required connection config aborts config-generation / startup loudly: a discovery.cache_ttl_ms or per_provider_timeout_ms <= 0, a secret_ref.mode:env whose env_key is absent from infrastructure.secret_keys, or an enabled non-ollama model with no llm.model_costs entry each aborts non-zero with a NAMED missing-config error and NO fallback-default value is substituted (G028, no silent Ollama fallback)

Connection credentials are stored protected at rest and NEVER returned in plaintext: the SecretVault AES-256-GCM-encrypts the secret bundle under the in-core master key with a per-record 96-bit nonce + AAD(connection_id|kind|key_version), persists only ciphertext+nonce+key_version+last-4 redaction to model_provider_connections, decrypts in-core only, and a re-read exposes presence+last-4+last-tested but never the secret; an AAD-tampered or wrong-key ciphertext is rejected (authenticated), and the credential is reversible (replayable to Authorization: Bearer), NOT one-way hashed

The open-knowledge /ask agent dispatch is provider-aware: with a hosted model selected, _dispatch_live takes the hosted branch (composes the litellm provider model <kind>/<backend-id> + api_base + the per-request cleartext api_key the Go core decrypted) and the answer is produced by the selected hosted model, not Ollama; the Go llm.ChatRequest and Python ChatRequest (extra=forbid) carry provider/api_base/api_key/provider_params additively

No selection (and only Ollama effective-enabled) keeps today's path byte-for-byte: _dispatch_live's ollama branch builds model=ollama_chat/<model>, api_base=OLLAMA_URL, NO api_key, with the litellm.acompletion kwargs IDENTICAL to today's _dispatch_live; an ADVERSARIAL parity test asserts the kwargs dict for a fixed Ollama request is unchanged and fails if any provider field leaks into the Ollama path (spec 088 IsZero() baseline)

A misconfigured / unreachable connection that is exercised fails loud and is NEVER silently re-routed to Ollama: when the hosted branch has a required api_key absent it returns a typed llm_misconfigured error (no Ollama substitution), and the DispatchResolver refuses a target whose connection is not effective-enabled with a typed reason rather than a silent local fallback

Every produced answer is attributed to the provider + model that generated it: the dispatch path carries the provider-qualified model (anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet) + selection source into the existing 089 ModelAttribution / TurnResult.Model contract, so attribution strings become provider-qualified and never silently coerced to a bare or Ollama name

Provider secrets never leak through the dispatch seam: _dispatch_live MUST NOT log api_key, and the error detail that crosses the wire is built from type(e).__name__ + provider + model with any api_key substring scrubbed (litellm exceptions can embed the key in a URL/header); an ADVERSARIAL test asserts the cleartext key never appears in any log line, span, or HTTP error body

Discovery aggregates Ollama-installed models (live GET /api/tags) and every effective-enabled hosted connection's models (SST-curated) into ONE provider-qualified ModelCatalog, each ModelDescriptor carrying id (<kind>/<backend-id>) + connection_id + kind + capabilities (tool_capable, vision, context_window); a slow/unreachable/auth-failed provider degrades gracefully (its models absent) and ALWAYS emits a typed ProviderDiscoveryStatus, the reachable subset always served, bounded by SST cache_ttl_ms + per_provider_timeout_ms

Selecting a model absent from the current catalog is refused closed-set with the SAME modelswitch.Rejection shape (the catalog is the validator's INJECTED admissible set, the leaf stays pure): canonicalization at the agenttool boundary normalizes a bare Ollama id to ollama/<id> iff installed, then the validator string-compares against the provider-qualified catalog; an off-catalog id yields a typed rejection with NO store write and NO dispatch

Selecting a paid hosted model enforces the per-user + global USD budgets BEFORE any billable provider call: the model-aware CostFn returns $0 for ollama (budget not consumed) and the SST llm.model_costs rate for a paid model, a missing rate for a billable model is a fail-loud typed refusal (NEVER a silent $0), and the existing agent.go budget pre-flight refuses an exhausted per-user/global ceiling with ErrCapUSDPerUserMonth/ErrCapUSDMonthly before dispatch, then appends actual cost to model_usage_ledger after a successful call

The operator adds an Anthropic connection and tests it through the operator-gated /v1/admin/model-connections* surface: PUT .../{id}/credential write-only-stores the secret (redacted view returned, never echoed), POST .../{id}/test runs a live reachability+credential probe via the per-kind adapter and reports a TRUTHFUL typed pass/fail, and on pass the connection can be enabled into discovery; only the operator identity may reach the surface

The operator adds OpenAI, Microsoft Foundry/Azure, Google, and Amazon Bedrock connections, each saved with its provider-specific write-only secret fields (non-secret params shown read-only from the SST registry) and each independently testable / enable-able / disable-able through the admin surface; the add flow is scoped to declared-but-unconfigured db-mode slots (a brand-new kind is an SST topology edit, not a UI invention)

The operator enables and disables a connection through POST .../{id}/enable | .../{id}/disable: enabling (409-guarded: only when a credential is present AND last test = ok) adds the provider's models to the combined catalog, disabling removes them; the effective-enabled predicate (registry-declared AND DB enabled AND last_test_outcome=ok AND credential present) is the single gate discovery consults

A failed test connection reports a typed, actionable error and NEVER a false success and NEVER substitutes Ollama: an invalid key / unreachable endpoint yields outcome:failed with a typed detail (auth_failed | unreachable | timeout) naming the connection, last_test_outcome is persisted failed (never the secret), and the slot cannot be enabled into the catalog (409 guard) — an ADVERSARIAL test fails if a failed probe is ever reported ok

A user lists the combined models in Telegram (the existing /model numbered picker) and picks a hosted model: the picker renders one provider-grouped, provider-qualified, cost-hinted list (Ollama/local group first), only reachable models are numbered so a numbered reply always maps to a dispatchable model, an unreachable provider is shown-but-disabled with its typed status (never dropped), and selecting a hosted model persists it via the existing claim-bound modelpref store

The same combined catalog and selection are available over HTTP/web (/v1/agent/model) and resolve through the SAME validator and SAME store as Telegram (one-validator/one-store parity, SCN-089 precedent): GET returns the provider-qualified catalog with additive per-entry capabilities[] + cost_class enrichment (allowed_models[] preserved byte-for-byte for 089 clients), PUT accepts the same selection Telegram accepts, and both surfaces render the same result

A user's selection persists across turns with per-request > sticky > default precedence preserved provider-agnostically: a sticky hosted selection set on either surface is returned on subsequent /ask turns until changed, a per-request override beats the sticky for one invocation only, and the precedence ordering + the modelpref store reads are byte-for-byte the 089 contract with the only change being provider-qualified ids

Specs 088/089 fork, precedence, and parity are preserved provider-agnostically: a user switches the gather model and the synthesis model independently, the gather-vs-synthesis turn fork + the per-request>sticky>default precedence + the one-validator/one-store parity behave EXACTLY as 088/089 define, and the only change is that the selected model may now belong to any connected provider; an ADVERSARIAL parity test fails if a second validator/store is introduced or if a non-tool-capable gather selection is accepted
